steps.title: Track the narrator's warning
steps.content: The narrator says it was fortunate Barton did not hear his neighbors' conversation and asks whether anyone would benefit from comparing self-image with others' opinions. The metaphor suggests people rely on flattering self-conceptions.

steps.title: Separate nearby themes
steps.content: A focuses on fixation with approval, B on obstacles to generosity, and C on openly criticizing others. The passage instead says people are protected by not knowing how others see them. The answer is D.
